pages.raw_text:
CHICAGO, June 27.-The organiza-
ton of an international labor union
whose trend shall follow the teach
ings Of socialism, Is the purpose of a
gathering of labor leaders which as


sembled in Chicago today. The king


pin of the movement is Eugene V.
Debs. the organizer of the now de-


funct American Railway union and


late candidate for president Of the
United States on the socialst-dem
cratic ticket. The new organization
is to be launched under the name of
the Industrial union.


The new movement makes no con-
cealment of it's antagonism to exist
ing labor organizations and methods,
particularly the American Federation
of Labor and the United Mine Work.
ers. After attacking the present sys-
tenn of labor organization and pro-
hibitive initiation fees, the official
call for the convention which assem-
bled today makes the declaration that
GLASS divisions foster political ig-
norance among workers, thus divid-
ing them at the ballot box as well
as nthe shop, mine and factory"
Continuing the call says:


Graft unions may and have been
used to assist employers in the es-
Enlistment of monopolies and the
raising of prices. They permit the
association of the misleaders of the
workers with the capitalists in the
Civic federation, where plans are
made for the perpetuation of capital
ism and the permanent enslavement
of the workers through the wage sys'
tenn. A movement to meet these con-
ditions must consist of one great in
dustrial union. embracing all indus
tries. providing for craft autonomy
locally. industrial autonomy interna-
tionally and working-cla unity gen.
orally. 1t should be founded on the
class struggle"
